title: Berry Flux Equals Topological Charge
summary: For an isolated bad point, the integral of Berry curvature over a small surrounding sphere equals the integer topological charge of the node.
assumptions: The Berry connection is defined on the punctured sphere around the node.
regime: Abelian Berry geometry of an isolated node.
scope: Lecture One Berry-curvature restatement of node charge.

source_anchors.notes: The flux formula and dF=0 turn local geometry into a global sum rule.

representation: Surface-flux formula for node charge in terms of Berry curvature.

symbols.meaning: integer charge of the bad point

failure_modes: The simple abelian formula changes when multiple occupied bands require a non-abelian treatment.
retrieval_hints: Use when translating geometric Berry data into topological charge.
